rationale: Title X is stronger for HR because it states the method, clinical use, validation size, cutoff, and diagnostic performance. It is slightly dense, but the specificity makes it more compelling and useful for readers scanning the table of contents. Title Y is clean and journal appropriate, yet it reads generic and does not communicate the key threshold, cohort, or strength of the result.
loser_weakness: Title Y lacks the main quantitative finding and validation detail, so it undersells the study and could describe a broader methods paper without a clear result.
